geopolitics_summary: Strait of Hormuz tensions and UK sanctions rollback risk crude/LNG supply disruption; European heatwave elevates summer demand risk.

themes.headline: Strait of Hormuz tensions escalate; NATO considers stabilisation role.
themes.why_it_matters: Hormuz closure threat reduces Middle East crude/LNG supply to EU, raising TTF via fuel-switching economics and pushing power curve higher if gas supply tightens.

themes.headline: Europe records record May heatwave; demand surge signals summer load risk.
themes.why_it_matters: Heat-driven cooling demand boosts day-ahead and Cal+1 power prices; strains hydro reserves if heatwave persists; reduces wind generation in southern Europe.

themes.headline: EU energy security threatened by Hormuz crisis; ministers convene.
themes.why_it_matters: Policy response may trigger LNG procurement acceleration or reserve builds; upward pressure on TTF and power if supply-side hedging accelerates.

themes.headline: UK rolls back Russia sanctions; EU objects to timing and scope.
themes.why_it_matters: UK sanction rollback could ease Russian energy re-entry into UK market; indirect EU TTF effect via LNG arb shifts if UK demand pulls Atlantic cargoes.

themes.headline: US plans 44.9 Bcf/d new gas pipeline capacity for 2026–2027.
themes.why_it_matters: Increased US export capacity boosts LNG supply to Europe, easing TTF; downward pressure on power if gas becomes cheaper fuel for generation.

watchlist: Strait of Hormuz: NATO meeting outcomes (late May), military escalation risk.
European heatwave: extend/fade forecast for June; hydro storage impact tracking.
UK-Russia sanctions: EU counter-measures or trade policy response timing.
COP31 (Turkey host): climate electrification agenda; possible EU ETS tightening signals.
